headline: Jobs growth cools sharply - only 57K payrolls added
detail: 57K is well below typical monthly prints and signals real economic slowdown. Soft labor markets can hurt revenue outlooks for enterprise tech and chip demand cycles. For your SMH, SOXL, and QQQ-heavy book, this raises recession risk which hits high-beta tech disproportionately, especially on margin.
rec: No new action triggered directly, but watch SMH - if it breaks toward your $540 stop, that's the sector telling you the macro fear is winning over rate-cut hope. Hold stops, don't average down yet.

headline: Tesla sinking despite blowout deliveries
detail: When good news can't hold a stock up, it signals weak underlying sentiment in the high-growth tech/EV space. This is a read-across warning for your other momentum names - CRWD, SOXL, SMH. Markets are not rewarding beats right now.
rec: No specific trade, but this reinforces not adding to underwater positions today. Sentiment fragility in momentum names means your stops matter more than usual.

headline: Labor force participation at 50-year low outside Covid
detail: Structural labor market weakness compounds the soft payrolls number. Weak employment feeds into weaker consumer and enterprise spending, which flows through to cloud, software, and semiconductor demand. Your entire book is exposed.
rec: No action today - this is background pressure, not a one-day catalyst. But it strengthens the case for respecting stops across SOXL, QQQI, and SMH rather than hoping for recovery.

headline: Stock market gains minted nearly 1 million new millionaires in 2025
detail: UBS data showing wealth creation from market gains suggests institutional money has been participating in the rally. For your book, it's mild confirmation that the broader uptrend in QQQ and SPY is real - not a fluke. However, the jobs print today is a fresh risk that could reverse recent gains quickly.
rec: No trade here - your QQQ and SPY positions are nearly flat and holding above stops, so no reason to add or trim based on this headline alone.
